# MCP Tool Schemas

`get_blueprint_tools()` publishes **4 tool definitions**.

## `list_blueprints`

List available workflow blueprints (pre-built patterns). Call this FIRST to check if a blueprint matches the user's request before building a workflow from scratch.

Definition: [`flyto_blueprint/tools.py:6`](https://github.com/flytohub/flyto-blueprint/blob/main/flyto_blueprint/tools.py#L6).

| Input | Type | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `query` | `string` | no | Optional search query to filter blueprints by name, description, or tags |

## `use_blueprint`

Expand a workflow blueprint with provided arguments. Returns a complete workflow YAML ready to use. Call inspect_page() first to get real selectors, then call this with the blueprint ID and args.

Definition: [`flyto_blueprint/tools.py:24`](https://github.com/flytohub/flyto-blueprint/blob/main/flyto_blueprint/tools.py#L24).

| Input | Type | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blueprint_id` | `string` | yes | Blueprint ID (from list_blueprints) |
| `args` | `object` | yes | Arguments to fill into the blueprint |

## `save_as_blueprint`

Save a workflow as a reusable blueprint. Pass a complete workflow (with steps array) and the engine will abstract concrete values into parameters, creating a reusable pattern.

Definition: [`flyto_blueprint/tools.py:48`](https://github.com/flytohub/flyto-blueprint/blob/main/flyto_blueprint/tools.py#L48).

| Input | Type | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `workflow` | `object` | yes | Workflow dict with steps array |
| `name` | `string` | no | Blueprint name |
| `tags` | `array` | no | Tags for search |

## `report_blueprint_outcome`

Report whether a blueprint-generated workflow succeeded or failed. Call this after the user runs a workflow that was built from a blueprint.

Definition: [`flyto_blueprint/tools.py:76`](https://github.com/flytohub/flyto-blueprint/blob/main/flyto_blueprint/tools.py#L76).

| Input | Type | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `blueprint_id` | `string` | yes | Blueprint ID that was used |
| `success` | `boolean` | yes | True if the workflow succeeded, false if it failed |
